revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese Buddhist Architecture}} {{italic title}} [[File:Zenkouji7835.jpg|thumb|x200px|The ''kyōzō'' of [[Zenkō-ji]] temple]] {{nihongo||経蔵|'''''Kyōzō'''''}} in [[Japanese Buddhist architecture]] is a repository for [[sūtra]]s and chronicles of the temple history. It is also called {{nihongo||経庫|''kyōko''}}, {{nihongo||経堂|''kyō-dō''}}, or {{nihongo||蔵殿|''zōden''}}.<ref name=jaanus>{{Jaanus2|k/kyouzou|Kyouzou|June 14, 2011}}</ref> In ancient times the ''kyōzō'' was placed opposite the [[shōrō|belfry]] on the east–west axis of the temple. The earliest extant ''kyōzō'' is at [[Hōryū-ji]], and it is a two-storied structure.<ref name="jaanus"/> An example of one-storied ''kyōzō'' is at [[Tōshōdai-ji]] in [[Nara, Nara|Nara]]. A ''kyōzō's'' usual size is 3 x 3 [[ken (architecture)|''ken'']].
All storage buildings are equipped with shelving to store the containers that hold the rolled sūtras. Some temples have circular revolving shelves for sūtra storage: a central pillar revolves, like a vertical [[axle]], and octahedral tubes are attached to it. A revolving sūtra storage case is called {{nihongo|''rinzō''|輪蔵|extra=wheel repository}}.<ref name="jaanus"/> Revolving shelves are convenient because they allow [[priest]]s and [[monk]]s to select the needed sūtra quickly. Eventually, in some ''kyōzō'' the faithful were permitted to push the shelves around the pillar while praying—it was believed that they could receive religious edification without reading the sūtras.<ref name="jaanus"/>
Some scripture houses are [[National Treasures of Japan]]: * The ''kyōzō'' of [[Tōshōdai-ji]] * The ''kyōzō'' of [[Hōryū-ji]] * The ''kyōzō'' of [[Ankoku-ji (Takayama)|Ankoku-ji]]
==Gallery== {| |[[File:Horyuji-L0344.jpg|thumb|x140px|The ''kyōzō'' at [[Hōryū-ji]], 8th century]] |[[File:Toshodaiji Nara Nara pref09n4500.jpg|thumb|x140px|The ''kyōzō'' at [[Tōshōdai-ji]], 8th century]] |[[File:Zenkoji Temple Kyozo June 27 2007.jpg|thumb|x140px|Revolving shelves at [[Zenkō-ji]]]] |[[File:Kamakura Hasedera Rotating prayer wheel.jpg|thumb|x148px|''Rinzō'' at [[Hase-dera (Kamakura)|Hase-dera]]]] |}
==References== <references/> {{commons Category:kk]]
[[Category|Kyōzō}} {{Japanese architectural elements}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kyōzō]][[de:Kyōzō]][[en:Kyōzō]][[es:Kyōzō]][[fr:Kyōzō]][[ja:経蔵 (建築)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1325051]] Wikidata name: kyōzō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese Buddhist Architecture}} {{italic title}} [[File:Zenkouji7835.jpg|thumb|x200px|The ''kyōzō'' of [[:en:Zenkō-ji|Zenkō-ji]] temple]] {{nihongo||経蔵|'''''Kyōzō'''''}} in [[:en:Japanese Buddhist architecture|Japanese Buddhist architecture]] is a repository for [[:en:Sutra|Sutra]]s and chronicles of the temple history. It is also called {{nihongo||経庫|''kyōko''}}, {{nihongo||経堂|''kyō-dō''}}, or {{nihongo||蔵殿|''zōden''}}.<ref name=jaanus>{{Jaanus2|k/kyouzou|Kyouzou|June 14, 2011}}</ref> In ancient times the ''kyōzō'' was placed opposite the [[shōrō|belfry]] on the east–west axis of the temple. The earliest extant ''kyōzō'' is at [[Hōryū-ji]], and it is a two-storied structure.<ref name="jaanus"/> An example of one-storied ''kyōzō'' is at [[:en:Tōshōdai-ji|Tōshōdai-ji]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]. A ''kyōzō's'' usual size is 3 x 3 [[:en:Ken (unit)|''ken'']].
All storage buildings are equipped with shelving to store the containers that hold the rolled sūtras. Some temples have circular revolving shelves for sūtra storage: a central pillar revolves, like a vertical [[:en:Axle|Axle]], and octahedral tubes are attached to it. A revolving sūtra storage case is called {{nihongo|''rinzō''|輪蔵|extra=wheel repository}}.<ref name="jaanus"/> Revolving shelves are convenient because they allow [[:en:Priest|Priest]]s and [[:en:Monk|Monk]]s to select the needed sūtra quickly. Eventually, in some ''kyōzō'' the faithful were permitted to push the shelves around the pillar while praying—it was believed that they could receive religious edification without reading the sūtras.<ref name="jaanus"/>
Some scripture houses are [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure (Japan)]]: * The ''kyōzō'' of [[:en:Tōshōdai-ji|Tōshōdai-ji]] * The ''kyōzō'' of [[Hōryū-ji]] * The ''kyōzō'' of [[Ankoku-ji (Takayama)|Ankoku-ji]]
==Gallery== {| |[[File:Horyuji-L0344.jpg|thumb|x140px|The ''kyōzō'' at [[Hōryū-ji]], 8th century]] |[[File:Toshodaiji Nara Nara pref09n4500.jpg|thumb|x140px|The ''kyōzō'' at [[:en:Tōshōdai-ji|Tōshōdai-ji]], 8th century]] |[[File:Zenkoji Temple Kyozo June 27 2007.jpg|thumb|x140px|Revolving shelves at [[:en:Zenkō-ji|Zenkō-ji]]]] |[[File:Kamakura Hasedera Rotating prayer wheel.jpg|thumb|x148px|''Rinzō'' at [[:en:Hase-dera (Kamakura)|Hase-dera]]]] |}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kyōzō]][[de:Kyōzō]][[en:Kyōzō]][[es:Kyōzō]][[fr:Kyōzō]][[ja:経蔵 (建築)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1325051]] Wikidata name: kyōzō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese Buddhist Architecture}} {{italic title}} [[File:Zenkouji7835.jpg|thumb|x200px|The ''kyōzō'' of [[:en:Zenkō-ji|Zenkō-ji]] temple]] {{nihongo||経蔵|'''''Kyōzō'''''}} in [[:en:Japanese Buddhist architecture|Japanese Buddhist architecture]] is a repository for [[:en:Sutra|Sutra]]s and chronicles of the temple history. It is also called {{nihongo||経庫|''kyōko''}}, {{nihongo||経堂|''kyō-dō''}}, or {{nihongo||蔵殿|''zōden''}}.<ref name=jaanus>{{Jaanus2|k/kyouzou|Kyouzou|June 14, 2011}}</ref> In ancient times the ''kyōzō'' was placed opposite the [[shōrō|belfry]] on the east–west axis of the temple. The earliest extant ''kyōzō'' is at [[Hōryū-ji]], and it is a two-storied structure.<ref name="jaanus"/> An example of one-storied ''kyōzō'' is at [[:en:Tōshōdai-ji|Tōshōdai-ji]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]. A ''kyōzō's'' usual size is 3 x 3 [[:en:Ken (unit)|''ken'']].
All storage buildings are equipped with shelving to store the containers that hold the rolled sūtras. Some temples have circular revolving shelves for sūtra storage: a central pillar revolves, like a vertical [[:en:Axle|Axle]], and octahedral tubes are attached to it. A revolving sūtra storage case is called {{nihongo|''rinzō''|輪蔵|extra=wheel repository}}.<ref name="jaanus"/> Revolving shelves are convenient because they allow [[:en:Priest|Priest]]s and [[:en:Monk|Monk]]s to select the needed sūtra quickly. Eventually, in some ''kyōzō'' the faithful were permitted to push the shelves around the pillar while praying—it was believed that they could receive religious edification without reading the sūtras.<ref name="jaanus"/>
Some scripture houses are [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure (Japan)]]: * The ''kyōzō'' of [[:en:Tōshōdai-ji|Tōshōdai-ji]] * The ''kyōzō'' of [[Hōryū-ji]] * The ''kyōzō'' of [[Ankoku-ji (Takayama)|Ankoku-ji]]
==Gallery== {| |[[File:Horyuji-L0344.jpg|thumb|x140px|The ''kyōzō'' at [[Hōryū-ji]], 8th century]] |[[File:Toshodaiji Nara Nara pref09n4500.jpg|thumb|x140px|The ''kyōzō'' at [[:en:Tōshōdai-ji|Tōshōdai-ji]], 8th century]] |[[File:Zenkoji Temple Kyozo June 27 2007.jpg|thumb|x140px|Revolving shelves at [[:en:Zenkō-ji|Zenkō-ji]]]] |[[File:Kamakura Hasedera Rotating prayer wheel.jpg|thumb|x148px|''Rinzō'' at [[:en:Hase-dera (Kamakura)|Hase-dera]]]] |}
==References== <references/> {{commons Category:kk]]

Eventually, in some ''kyōzō'' the faithful were permitted to push the shelves around the pillar while praying—it was believed that they could receive religious edification without reading the sūtras.<ref name="jaanus"/>

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kyōzō]][[de:Kyōzō]][[en:Kyōzō]][[es:Kyōzō]][[fr:Kyōzō]][[ja:経蔵 (建築)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kyōzō]][[de:Kyōzō]][[en:Kyōzō]][[es:Kyōzō]][[fr:Kyōzō]][[ja:経蔵 (建築)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kyōzō]][[de:Kyōzō]][[en:Kyōzō]][[es:Kyōzō]][[fr:Kyōzō]][[ja:経蔵 (建築)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1325051]] Wikidata name: kyōzō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese Buddhist Architecture}} {{italic title}} [[File:Zenkouji7835.jpg|thumb|x200px|The ''kyōzō'' of {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkō-ji}} temple]] {{nihongo||経蔵|'''''Kyōzō'''''}} in {{ill|Japanese Buddhist architecture|en|Japanese Buddhist architecture|WD=Q7883744|lt=Japanese Buddhist architecture}} is a repository for {{ill|Sutra|en|Sutra|WD=Q209680|lt=Sutra}}s and chronicles of the temple history. It is also called {{nihongo||経庫|''kyōko''}}, {{nihongo||経堂|''kyō-dō''}}, or {{nihongo||蔵殿|''zōden''}}.<ref name=jaanus>{{Jaanus2|k/kyouzou|Kyouzou|June 14, 2011}}</ref> In ancient times the ''kyōzō'' was placed opposite the [[shōrō|belfry]] on the east–west axis of the temple. The earliest extant ''kyōzō'' is at [[Hōryū-ji]], and it is a two-storied structure.<ref name="jaanus"/> An example of one-storied ''kyōzō'' is at {{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}. A ''kyōzō's'' usual size is 3 x 3 {{ill|Ken (unit)|en|Ken (unit)|WD=Q3195007|lt=''ken''}}.
All storage buildings are equipped with shelving to store the containers that hold the rolled sūtras. Some temples have circular revolving shelves for sūtra storage: a central pillar revolves, like a vertical {{ill|Axle|en|Axle|WD=Q188209|lt=Axle}}, and octahedral tubes are attached to it. A revolving sūtra storage case is called {{nihongo|''rinzō''|輪蔵|extra=wheel repository}}.<ref name="jaanus"/> Revolving shelves are convenient because they allow {{ill|Priest|en|Priest|WD=Q42603|lt=Priest}}s and {{ill|Monk|en|Monk|WD=Q733786|lt=Monk}}s to select the needed sūtra quickly. Eventually, in some ''kyōzō'' the faithful were permitted to push the shelves around the pillar while praying—it was believed that they could receive religious edification without reading the sūtras.<ref name="jaanus"/>
Some scripture houses are {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure (Japan)}}: * The ''kyōzō'' of {{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}} * The ''kyōzō'' of [[Hōryū-ji]] * The ''kyōzō'' of [[Ankoku-ji (Takayama)|Ankoku-ji]]
==Gallery== {| |[[File:Horyuji-L0344.jpg|thumb|x140px|The ''kyōzō'' at [[Hōryū-ji]], 8th century]] |[[File:Toshodaiji Nara Nara pref09n4500.jpg|thumb|x140px|The ''kyōzō'' at {{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}}, 8th century]] |[[File:Zenkoji Temple Kyozo June 27 2007.jpg|thumb|x140px|Revolving shelves at {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkō-ji}}]] |[[File:Kamakura Hasedera Rotating prayer wheel.jpg|thumb|x148px|''Rinzō'' at {{ill|Hase-dera (Kamakura)|en|Hase-dera (Kamakura)|WD=Q1034293|lt=Hase-dera}}]] |}

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Removing from [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kyōzō]][[de:Kyōzō]][[en:Kyōzō]][[es:Kyōzō]][[fr:Kyōzō]][[ja:経蔵 (建築)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kyōzō]][[de:Kyōzō]][[en:Kyōzō]][[es:Kyōzō]][[fr:Kyōzō]][[ja:経蔵 (建築)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kyōzō]][[de:Kyōzō]][[en:Kyōzō]][[es:Kyōzō]][[fr:Kyōzō]][[ja:経蔵 (建築)]]
{{Short description|Japanese Buddhist Architecture}} {{italic title}} [[File:Zenkouji7835.jpg|thumb|x200px|The ''kyōzō'' of {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkō-ji}} temple]] {{nihongo||経蔵|'''''Kyōzō'''''}} in {{ill|Japanese Buddhist architecture|en|Japanese Buddhist architecture|WD=Q7883744|lt=Japanese Buddhist architecture}} is a repository for {{ill|Sutra|en|Sutra|WD=Q209680|lt=Sutra}}s and chronicles of the temple history. It is also called {{nihongo||経庫|''kyōko''}}, {{nihongo||経堂|''kyō-dō''}}, or {{nihongo||蔵殿|''zōden''}}.<ref name=jaanus>{{Jaanus2|k/kyouzou|Kyouzou|June 14, 2011}}</ref> In ancient times the ''kyōzō'' was placed opposite the [[shōrō|belfry]] on the east–west axis of the temple. The earliest extant ''kyōzō'' is at [[Hōryū-ji]], and it is a two-storied structure.<ref name="jaanus"/> An example of one-storied ''kyōzō'' is at {{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}. A ''kyōzō's'' usual size is 3 x 3 {{ill|Ken (unit)|en|Ken (unit)|WD=Q3195007|lt=''ken''}}.
All storage buildings are equipped with shelving to store the containers that hold the rolled sūtras. Some temples have circular revolving shelves for sūtra storage: a central pillar revolves, like a vertical {{ill|Axle|en|Axle|WD=Q188209|lt=Axle}}, and octahedral tubes are attached to it. A revolving sūtra storage case is called {{nihongo|''rinzō''|輪蔵|extra=wheel repository}}.<ref name="jaanus"/> Revolving shelves are convenient because they allow {{ill|Priest|en|Priest|WD=Q42603|lt=Priest}}s and {{ill|Monk|en|Monk|WD=Q733786|lt=Monk}}s to select the needed sūtra quickly. Eventually, in some ''kyōzō'' the faithful were permitted to push the shelves around the pillar while praying—it was believed that they could receive religious edification without reading the sūtras.<ref name="jaanus"/>
Some scripture houses are {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure (Japan)}}: * The ''kyōzō'' of {{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}} * The ''kyōzō'' of [[Hōryū-ji]] * The ''kyōzō'' of [[Ankoku-ji (Takayama)|Ankoku-ji]]
==Gallery== {| |[[File:Horyuji-L0344.jpg|thumb|x140px|The ''kyōzō'' at [[Hōryū-ji]], 8th century]] |[[File:Toshodaiji Nara Nara pref09n4500.jpg|thumb|x140px|The ''kyōzō'' at {{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}}, 8th century]] |[[File:Zenkoji Temple Kyozo June 27 2007.jpg|thumb|x140px|Revolving shelves at {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkō-ji}}]] |[[File:Kamakura Hasedera Rotating prayer wheel.jpg|thumb|x148px|''Rinzō'' at {{ill|Hase-dera (Kamakura)|en|Hase-dera (Kamakura)|WD=Q1034293|lt=Hase-dera}}]] |}
==References== <references/> {{commons Category:kk]]

==Gallery== {| |[[File:Horyuji-L0344.jpg|thumb|x140px|The ''kyōzō'' at [[Hōryū-ji]], 8th century]] |[[File:Toshodaiji Nara Nara pref09n4500.jpg|thumb|x140px|The ''kyōzō'' at {{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji}}, 8th century]] |[[File:Zenkoji Temple Kyozo June 27 2007.jpg|thumb|x140px|Revolving shelves at {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkō-ji}}]] |[[File:Kamakura Hasedera Rotating prayer wheel.jpg|thumb|x148px|''Rinzō'' at {{ill|Hase-dera (Kamakura)|en|Hase-dera (Kamakura)|WD=Q1034293|lt=Hase-dera}}]] |}

{{Short description|Japanese Buddhist Architecture}} {{italic title}} [[File:Zenkouji7835.jpg|thumb|x200px|The ''kyōzō'' of {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkō-ji|1=Zenkō-ji}} temple]] {{nihongo||経蔵|'''''Kyōzō'''''}} in {{ill|Japanese Buddhist architecture|en|Japanese Buddhist architecture|WD=Q7883744|lt=Japanese Buddhist architecture|1=Japanese Buddhist architecture}} is a repository for {{ill|Sutra|en|Sutra|WD=Q209680|lt=Sutra|1=Sutra}}s and chronicles of the temple history. It is also called {{nihongo||経庫|''kyōko''}}, {{nihongo||経堂|''kyō-dō''}}, or {{nihongo||蔵殿|''zōden''}}.<ref name=jaanus>{{Jaanus2|k/kyouzou|Kyouzou|June 14, 2011}}</ref> In ancient times the ''kyōzō'' was placed opposite the [[shōrō|belfry]] on the east–west axis of the temple. The earliest extant ''kyōzō'' is at [[Hōryū-ji]], and it is a two-storied structure.<ref name="jaanus"/> An example of one-storied ''kyōzō'' is at {{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji|1=Tōshōdai-ji}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}. A ''kyōzō's'' usual size is 3 x 3 {{ill|Ken (unit)|en|Ken (unit)|WD=Q3195007|lt=''ken''|1=Ken (unit)}}.
All storage buildings are equipped with shelving to store the containers that hold the rolled sūtras. Some temples have circular revolving shelves for sūtra storage: a central pillar revolves, like a vertical {{ill|Axle|en|Axle|WD=Q188209|lt=Axle|1=Axle}}, and octahedral tubes are attached to it. A revolving sūtra storage case is called {{nihongo|''rinzō''|輪蔵|extra=wheel repository}}.<ref name="jaanus"/> Revolving shelves are convenient because they allow {{ill|Priest|en|Priest|WD=Q42603|lt=Priest|1=Priest}}s and {{ill|Monk|en|Monk|WD=Q733786|lt=Monk|1=Monk}}s to select the needed sūtra quickly. Eventually, in some ''kyōzō'' the faithful were permitted to push the shelves around the pillar while praying—it was believed that they could receive religious edification without reading the sūtras.<ref name="jaanus"/>
Some scripture houses are {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure (Japan)|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}: * The ''kyōzō'' of {{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji|1=Tōshōdai-ji}} * The ''kyōzō'' of [[Hōryū-ji]] * The ''kyōzō'' of [[Ankoku-ji (Takayama)|Ankoku-ji]]
==Gallery== {| |[[File:Horyuji-L0344.jpg|thumb|x140px|The ''kyōzō'' at [[Hōryū-ji]], 8th century]] |[[File:Toshodaiji Nara Nara pref09n4500.jpg|thumb|x140px|The ''kyōzō'' at {{ill|Tōshōdai-ji|en|Tōshōdai-ji|WD=Q973175|lt=Tōshōdai-ji|1=Tōshōdai-ji}}, 8th century]] |[[File:Zenkoji Temple Kyozo June 27 2007.jpg|thumb|x140px|Revolving shelves at {{ill|Zenkō-ji|en|Zenkō-ji|WD=Q189640|lt=Zenkō-ji|1=Zenkō-ji}}]] |[[File:Kamakura Hasedera Rotating prayer wheel.jpg|thumb|x148px|''Rinzō'' at {{ill|Hase-dera (Kamakura)|en|Hase-dera (Kamakura)|WD=Q1034293|lt=Hase-dera|1=Hase-dera (Kamakura)}}]] |}
==References== <references/> {{commons Category:kk]]
